steps.title: Identify the key contrast
steps.content: Traditional forms like the novel are “recognizable as such”—clearly defined and bounded. The blank must describe forms that do the opposite: they “borrow elements from both fables and realist narratives” and “straddle the line between prose and poetry,” resisting any single clear definition.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: ”Amorphous” means lacking a clear, fixed form—the quality these blended forms possess. The other choices don't fit the context: “dispersed” refers to scattering, “customary” contradicts the unconventional nature, and “neutral” is unrelated. That matches choice A — A is correct.
